primary_accounting_issue: Whether foreign currency risk arising from an intragroup dividend-related receivable can be designated as a hedged item in consolidated financial statements under IFRS hedge accounting.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern whether intragroup items or forecast intragroup transactions can qualify as hedged items in consolidated financial statements.
These paragraphs define the top-level types of eligible hedged items and the basic qualification criteria for hedge accounting.

supporting_authority.reason: These paragraphs clarify when exchange differences on monetary items, including intragroup monetary balances, remain visible in consolidated financial statements.

These paragraphs provide a distinct OCI model for monetary items forming part of a net investment in a foreign operation, relevant as a separate hedge accounting model involving foreign currency risk.
These paragraphs confirm that foreign currency hedge accounting is governed by IFRS 9 rather than IAS 21, while IAS 21 still frames the underlying exchange-difference accounting.
This interpretation clarifies the separate hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk on net investments in foreign operations.

These paragraphs confirm that gains and losses on hedged items are recognised in accordance with hedge accounting requirements, supporting identification of hedge accounting as the relevant model family.

peripheral_authority.reason: These are disclosure requirements and do not determine the peer accounting models for designation of the hedged item.
These are consolidation and structured-entity disclosures, not recognition or hedge-accounting models for intragroup dividend receivables.
These are reduced-disclosure or presentation requirements and do not govern hedge designation.
These paragraphs address non-cash distributions to owners and dividend payable accounting, not hedge-accounting models for foreign currency risk in consolidation.
These paragraphs concern liability-versus-equity classification of members' shares and are unrelated to the hedge-accounting issue.
These paragraphs address cash flow statement classification and financing liability disclosures, not the recognition model for hedged items.
These paragraphs address provisions and contingent liabilities rather than hedge accounting.
This interpretation concerns tax-status changes and is unrelated to the identified hedge-accounting issue.
This interpretation concerns EMU changeover translation issues and does not identify peer hedge-accounting models for the question.
These paragraphs address other measurement, presentation, or specific designation topics that do not define the peer hedge-accounting models relevant here.

treatment_families.family: Hedge accounting for recognised items exposed to foreign currency risk
Hedge accounting for highly probable forecast foreign currency cash flows
Hedge accounting for foreign currency risk on a net investment in a foreign operation

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS identifies hedge accounting for recognised assets or liabilities as a distinct top-level model, which can be relevant where a dividend gives rise to a recognised receivable.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS identifies hedge accounting for forecast transactions as a separate top-level model, including highly probable forecast intragroup foreign currency transactions in limited circumstances.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS and IFRIC 16 treat hedging foreign currency risk on a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate hedge-accounting model.
